selfExtension.intro: Propose a self-extension for this installed plugin: add tools or capabilities by patching its spec. The escalation guard auto-denies any privilege widening — a plugin can never grant itself rights it does not already hold.
selfExtension.patchesPlaceholder: Spec patches as a JSON array, e.g. [{"op":"add","path":"/tools/-","value":{"id":"my_tool","description":"…","input":{}}}]
selfExtension.invalidPatchesJson: Patches must be a valid JSON array of RFC-6902 operations.
selfExtension.invalidParamsJson: Params must be a valid JSON object.

operatorChannels.title: Channels
operatorChannels.subtitle: Route inbound webhooks per channel-key to an Agent. Each channel plugin reports its own keys — you only pick which Agent answers them.
operatorChannels.emptyExplain: Channel plugins (Teams, Telegram, …) report their keys during activation. As soon as a channel plugin is installed and active, its keys appear here.
operatorChannels.fallbackHint: Channels without an explicit binding fall through to the fallback Agent ({fallback}).

layout.subtitle: Management UI · local
layout.metaDescription: Admin UI for the Omadia agent platform (local).

login.title: Sign in to Omadia
login.noProviders: No auth providers are configured. Set {envVar} in the middleware environment and restart.

setup.title: Welcome to Omadia
setup.intro: Create the first administrator account. You can invite more users from the admin UI afterwards.
setup.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
setup.alreadyLocked: Setup is already locked — another user has been created.
setup.credentialsRejected: Email or password rejected by the server (must be valid email + 8+ chars).
setup.anthropicKeyHelp: Starts with sk-ant-…. Get one at console.anthropic.com. Stored encrypted in the per-plugin vault — never in .env. Leave empty to add later via Admin → Runtime → Secrets.
setup.anthropicKeyInvalid: Anthropic API keys start with "sk-ant-". Double-check the value from console.anthropic.com.
setup.anthropicKeyRejected: The server rejected this Anthropic key (401/403). Verify it at console.anthropic.com → API keys.

onboarding.title: Pick a profile — operational in <60 seconds
onboarding.intro: This middleware is still empty ({count, plural, one {# plugin} other {# plugins}} installed). Pick a curated profile — the plugins will be registered in the correct capability order. Secrets like API keys are set afterwards via the wizard.
onboarding.exportSuffix: and import it into another deployment.
onboarding.secretsHint: Plugins that need operator secrets (e.g. anthropic_api_key, telegram_bot_token) start in status {erroredTag} until you set the secrets via the wizard on the respective detail page. Then activate via {reactivateTag}.

chatTabs.closeWhileBusyTitle: Wait until the turn is done

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: This page hit an unexpected error and couldn't finish loading.
error.resetDataHint: Clears chats stored locally in this browser, then reloads. Your server-side data is untouched — use this if reloading alone doesn't help.

session.warningBody: Your session ends in {time}. Sign in again now so you are not interrupted in the middle of your work.
session.expiredBody: Your session has expired. Please sign in again to continue working.

agentDetailsModal.footerNote: Top-level tool calls only. Inner agent iterations are not counted as separate calls.

chat.errorMiddlewareUnreachable: Middleware unreachable on localhost:3979. Is `npm run dev` running in the middleware folder?
chat.errorProxyFailure: Proxy error (HTTP {status}). Check the middleware logs.
chat.composerResetTitle: Reset chat (⌘⇧K) — history goes, memory stays
chat.resetConfirmBody: The conversation in this chat will be cleared and the agent forgets its current context. Memory and Knowledge-Graph entries are preserved.
chat.steerButtonTitle: Inject this message into the running turn (⌘↵ / Ctrl↵) — applied at the next step
chat.steerHint: Type to steer this turn — your message is folded in at the next step. Or press Stop.
chat.steerNoticeSent: Steering sent — it will be applied at the next step.
chat.steerNoticeEnded: The turn just finished — press Send to ask it as a new message.
chat.emptyStatePrompt: Ask the orchestrator — the answer streams live including tool calls.
chat.emptyStateShortcut: Shortcut: ⌘↵ / Ctrl↵ · new tab via „+ New chat"

autoPromoted.label: Saved as {kind} by Palaia
autoPromoted.discardTitle: Delete this auto-saved memory — the manual save button comes back
autoPromoted.discardConfirm: Discard the auto-saved memory? The manual save button will come back so you can re-save with your own classification.

saveAsMemory.buttonTitle: Promote this turn into a curated MemorableKnowledge entry
saveAsMemory.summaryPlaceholder: Short summary of what this memory captures…
saveAsMemory.excerptInsertTitle: Insert this excerpt into the summary

source.llmTitle: Pre-filled by the Palaia excerpt extractor

saveAsMemory.rationalePlaceholder: Why is this memorable? Context the agent should keep…
saveAsMemory.successHeadline: Saved. The memory is now visible on /memories.

privacyReceipt.summaryDatasets: {count, plural, one {# tool result} other {# tool results}} processed server-side
privacyReceipt.summaryMasked: {count, plural, one {# field masked} other {# fields masked}}
privacyReceipt.summaryIdentityOnWire: ⚠ {count, plural, one {# name to the model} other {# names to the model}}
privacyReceipt.explainer: Raw tool results stayed server-side behind the data-plane boundary — the model only ever saw an identity-free digest.
privacyReceipt.explainerBreach: You named a person in your request — that name reached the model in cleartext. The data fetched from tools stayed server-side behind the boundary.
privacyReceipt.summaryBypassed: {count, plural, one {# tool not masked} other {# tools not masked}}
privacyReceipt.explainerBypassed: The operator set Privacy Mode to »Bypass« for at least one plugin — its tool results reached the model unmasked. Used for trusted internal sources whose content the Privacy Shield cannot usefully digest structurally.

privacyOperator.heroLede: Inspect what the shield is detecting, tune the allowlist, and live-test inputs against the current detector pool.
privacyOperator.errorLoadingState: Failed to load the privacy state.
privacyOperator.tenantSelfSubtitle: Terms naming the tenant itself; tokenising them would mask the conversation's primary subject.
privacyOperator.tenantSelfFooter: Source: tenant_self_terms plugin config. Auto-fill from operator profile lands in v0.2.x.
privacyOperator.repoDefaultSubtitle: German office / HR topic-nouns shipped with the plugin to suppress NER false positives.
privacyOperator.overrideSubtitle: Free-form additions you want passed through unmasked. One term per line.
privacyOperator.overrideRuntimeOnly: ⚠ Runtime-only — changes revert on restart. Durable persistence lands in a follow-up slice.
privacyOperator.liveTestSubtitle: Run the full detector + allowlist + tokenise pipeline on arbitrary text. No persistence; just visibility.
privacyOperator.liveTestPlaceholder: Paste text to inspect what the shield would do…
privacyOperator.liveTestNoDetectorHits: No detections — payload passes through unchanged.
privacyOperator.liveTestNoAllowlistMatches: No terms suppressed by the allowlist.
privacyOperator.auditSubtitle: Forensic timeline of receipts emitted across turns.
privacyOperator.auditDeferred: Available in v0.2.x — requires receipt persistence.

agentPicker.label: Agent:
agentPicker.pinnedTooltip: Session is pinned. Use Operator → Agents → Drain to reassign.
agentPicker.emptyCta: No agents configured — open Operator → Agents
agentPicker.unavailableTitle: This session's Agent is no longer available
agentPicker.unavailableBody: The Agent "{slug}" was deleted or disabled. Pick how to recover this session.

operatorAgents.title: Agents
operatorAgents.subtitle: Multi-orchestrator runtime — create, edit, disable, drain, or kill Agents and watch the registry react in real time via the LISTEN/NOTIFY hot-reload bus.
operatorAgents.agentsEmpty: No agents yet. Create one above.
operatorAgents.drainTooltip: Clear snapshot on every session bound to this Agent — next turn re-captures from current config
operatorAgents.killConfirm: Kill all sessions for "{slug}"? History will be lost.
operatorAgents.rehydrateTooltip: Re-attaches every installed plugin to the current fallback Agent — restores day-1 capabilities.
operatorAgents.rehydrateConfirm: Re-attach every installed plugin to the fallback Agent? Existing plugin configs are preserved; newly added plugins start with empty config.
operatorAgents.catalogError: Failed to load the plugin catalog ({message}). The multi-select falls back to manual mode.
operatorAgents.multiInstanceFalseBadge: multi_instance: false — may be active on at most ONE agent
operatorAgents.bindingsEmpty: No bindings — unbound traffic lands on the fallback Agent.
operatorAgents.routingTesterHelp: Ask the server which Agent would handle an inbound webhook. Hits explicit bindings first, then the platform fallback.
operatorAgents.routingTesterNoMatch: No match — and no platform fallback configured.
operatorAgents.pluginsAvailableEmpty: All installed plugins are already attached.
operatorAgents.pluginsEnabledEmpty: Drag-and-drop or click "Attach" to add plugins.
operatorAgents.dependencyMissingTooltip: This plugin depends on "{parent}" — parent is not attached in the same column.
operatorAgents.fallbackStoreOnlyNotice: The fallback Agent always runs plugins with the global store config. Per-Agent overrides are disabled here — create a dedicated Agent if you need them.
operatorAgents.orphanDetachAllTooltip: Detach every stale plugin row (old ids from earlier deployments) at once.
operatorAgents.orphanExplain: Plugin ids from earlier seeds that no longer exist in the current catalog (typically a rename like de.byte5.agent.* → @omadia/*). Dropped on next Save unless you tick "Keep".

streamToasts.abortConfirmBody: The running response will be aborted immediately and can't be resumed.

issueReport.publicWarning: This text goes into the public repo. Secrets and personal data were removed automatically — please review before filing.
issueReport.errorNoApp: Direct filing is not configured on this instance. Please report manually via the GitHub tab.
issueReport.errorInvalidNumber: Please enter a valid issue number.

intro.title: Let's build your assistant
intro.subtitle: In three calm steps — describe it, review it, try it out. The technical side takes care of itself.

step1.title: Describe your assistant
step1.subtitle: Say in your own words what it should do for you.

step2.title: Your assistant
step2.subtitle: An overview in plain language — no jargon.

step3.title: Try it out
step3.subtitle: Ask a question and see how your assistant replies.

overview.whatPlaceholder: Once you've described above what your assistant should do, a short summary will appear here.
overview.readyTools: Ready. Can carry out {count, plural, one {# action} other {# actions}} for you.
overview.readyChat: Ready. Answers your questions in the chat.

persona.title: Adjust personality
persona.subtitle: How should your assistant sound? Friendly, factual, casual …

intake.heroTitle: What should your assistant be able to do?
intake.heroSubtitle: Just describe it in your own words. We'll handle the rest — you don't need to know anything technical.
intake.example1: An assistant that answers questions about our Confluence pages.
intake.example2: A helper that summarizes incoming emails.
intake.example3: An assistant for questions about our vacation policy.
intake.placeholderIdle: Describe in your own words what your assistant should do.
intake.errorChoiceFailed: Your choice could not be saved.
intake.errorChoiceFailedMessage: Your choice could not be saved: {message}

workspace.viewModeSimpleTitle: Guided no-code view — describe, review, try out
workspace.viewModeExtendedTitle: Full builder — spec, slots, source code, build status
workspace.viewModeLockedTitle: Please wait for the current reply to finish before switching views
workspace.installEditorWarnings: {count, plural, one {# editor warning} other {# editor warnings}}
workspace.installMissingCredentials: {count, plural, one {# missing test credential} other {# missing test credentials}}
workspace.templateTooltipEnabled: Switch template (lossless — only slots / tools / depends_on are template-specific)
workspace.templateTooltipDisabled: Template switch disabled — the draft already has slots, tools or `depends_on` entries that would be lost on switch. Create a new draft or clear those fields first.
workspace.autoFixTitleOn: Auto-Fix active: build/smoke failures automatically trigger a builder turn
workspace.autoFixTitleOff: Auto-Fix off: failures only show the "Fix with Builder" button
workspace.autoFixStoppedBody: The builder agent can't get past the same class of error. Auto-Fix was switched off automatically — review the code manually or turn the toggle back on once you've intervened.

form.descriptionPlaceholder: What does this tool do? Read by the agent as the description.

list.stuckTitle: Builder agent got stuck on this tool/slot — check manually

bulkImport.title: Import tools
bulkImport.previewEmpty: Paste input to see the preview.
bulkImport.invalidJson: Input is not valid JSON. YAML support is coming in the B.11+ cleanup.
bulkImport.collisionNote: Collisions are skipped — existing tools stay unchanged.

test.subtitle: Direct call against the current preview instance.
test.dismissHint: Esc or a click outside closes the modal.
test.emptyInput: Tool accepts empty input — press Run.
test.arrayJsonHint: Array — JSON syntax, commits on blur.
test.objectJsonHint: Object — JSON syntax, commits on blur.

schema.itemPropertiesHint: Item properties are edited in the nested builder below (depth {depth}).
schema.topLevelMustBeObject: Top level must be an object.
schema.jsonModeHint: Direct JSON-schema mode. When switching to the form view, unsupported patterns (oneOf, $ref, polymorphic) become read-only.
schema.noProperties: No properties — tool accepts an empty input object.

placeholder.idle: Describe what the agent should do. Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a line break.

empty.title: Tell the builder what you want to start with.
empty.example: e.g. „Build a Confluence reader agent with the tools page_read and page_search.“

chat.credentialsTooltip: Manage test credentials for the preview

placeholder.idle: Test the agent. Press Enter to send.

error.unknownTurn: Unknown error during the preview turn

chat.externalReadsHint: Preview does not run cross-integration lookups (spec.external_reads) — the agent correctly detects the missing service and throws. Publish the agent to the platform store to test the real data path.

agentStuck.title: Builder agent is stuck on slot {slot} ({attempts, plural, one {# attempt} other {# attempts}})
agentStuck.body: {summary}. Open the slot editor, review the errors manually and fix the code — the agent won't retry on its own.

empty.title: Test the preview agent.
empty.body: Spec + slots are recompiled before each turn if anything changed.

secrets.title: Test credentials

persistence.persistent: Values are stored encrypted in the vault and survive a restart. Clicking «Clear all» wipes them permanently.
persistence.ephemeral: Values live only in the server's memory and are gone on logout/restart.
persistence.unknown: Values are stored server-side in the PreviewSecretBuffer.
persistence.notForProduction: Not for production — installed plugins pull their credentials from the vault via RequiresWizard.

secrets.noFields: No setup_fields declared. Add some in the spec tab to test credentials.

placeholder.buffered: set — leave empty to keep the value

array.malformedTooltip: Entry is not a string — shown as best-effort

setupFields.emptyState: No setup fields yet. Add e.g. <example>api_key (secret, required)</example> so the preview agent can read test credentials.
setupFields.descriptionPlaceholder: What does the agent need this field for?

validation.keyFormat: Key must be snake_case (a-z, 0-9, _; start with a letter).

empty.title: No slots filled yet.
empty.hint: Let the Builder fill the slots, or write them yourself.

slots.askAgentTooltip: Sends the prompt straight to the Builder chat — the agent gets started right away.

pillar.heading: Shape character
pillar.description: 12 axes, each individually optional. Values are mirrored in the AGENT.md frontmatter — Phase 4 compiles them into a persona section in the system prompt.
pillar.customNotesPlaceholder: e.g. "Reply in German when the user writes in German."

gallery.title: Choose a persona template
gallery.customDescription: Apply no template — use your own axes.

culture.label: Industry / Culture
culture.noChange: No change — all values already match.

adminUi.title: Admin UI
adminUi.subtitle: An iframe in the store detail, operator-only. For maintaining data that does not come from the API.
adminUi.toggleHelp: Plugin mounts an HTML page at admin_ui_path. The Web UI embeds it as an <iframe> on the store detail page after install.
adminUi.bodyHint: Content between the marker regions. Inline style + script allowed.
adminUi.operatorContract: Operator contract: Frontend fetches must use relative URLs (fetch('api/devices'), not fetch('/api/...')) — the UI runs behind a /bot-api/* rewrite. JSON responses need the { ok: boolean, ... } schema, otherwise the RuntimeSmoke flags them as broken.
adminUi.disabledHint: Off. Plugins without admin_ui_path have no admin tile on the store detail page — that is the default.

dashboardPages.title: Dashboard Pages
dashboardPages.subtitle: Routes rendered as browser/Teams tabs. For end-user-facing displays (PR inbox, KPIs, lists).

tab.heading: UI Surfaces
tab.description: Expose plugin UI outward: one admin iframe for operator-maintained data (single), and 0–N dashboard pages for end-user-facing browser/Teams tab displays.

fields.routeIdHelp: Lowercase, hyphens allowed. Determines slot keys + filename.
fields.pathHelp: Relative to the plugin mount. Final: /p/<id><path>.
fields.tabLabelHelp: Max 24 characters. Shown in the Teams tab + hub card.
fields.pageTitleHelp: Full page title in the HTML doc. Max 80 characters.

renderMode.heading: Render Mode
renderMode.libraryDesc: Curated Tailwind templates (list-card / kpi-tiles). Fast, no build step.
renderMode.reactSsrDesc: TSX component (server-rendered). Full React DX, server-rendered, Tailwind via CDN.
renderMode.freeFormHtmlDesc: Free html`…` slot. You write the Express route body yourself.

uiTemplate.heading: UI Template
uiTemplate.listCardDesc: List of cards (title + subtitle + url). Default for "show me X items".
uiTemplate.kpiTilesDesc: 1–4 number tiles (label + value + optional hint). For counts/KPIs.

dataBinding.heading: Data Source
dataBinding.help: The selected tool is called with empty input on every page request; its output flows as props into the render function.

interactive.label: Interactive (Hydration)
interactive.help: SSR + client-side hydration via esm.sh import map. Set to true when your component uses useState, onClick, etc. The root element MUST carry data-omadia-page="{routeId}".

componentSlot.label: Component (TSX, default-export)
componentSlot.hint: Props: { data: unknown; fetchError: string | null }

itemTemplate.heading: Item Template (path interpolation: item.fieldname)
itemTemplate.suffixHelp: Whitelisted suffixes: .join(", ") for arrays, .toLocaleString() for numbers. Other function calls are ignored.

pagesList.emptyHint: A page becomes a /p/<plugin>/<path> endpoint and a hub card for Teams.

hints.previewCold: Click "Build" or send a chat message — once the preview is warm (green build status), the iframe loads automatically.
hints.uiRouteNotFound: The routeId does not exist in the current spec. Save your page first (same form), then try the reload again.
hints.previewCaptureMissing: The warm preview was built from a spec without ui_routes. Trigger a build (chat or "Rebuild") after creating the page.
hints.previewProxyFailed: The temporary probe server could not load the plugin route. Check the console: the component probably throws during SSR.
hints.fetchFailed: Network error while loading the preview. Try refresh.

versions.relDaysAgo: {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} ago
versions.emptyStateHint: No snapshots yet. “Create snapshot” pins the current state so you can roll back to it later.
versions.downloadPluginTitle: Plugin ZIP — directly installable via /install/packages/upload (incl. AGENT.md with persona/quality)
versions.downloadBundleTitle: Profile bundle — for cross-instance migration via /api/v1/profiles/import-bundle
versions.toastRollbackDone: Rollback complete · {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}} restored
versions.vendorBundleLabel: Pack plugin ZIPs into the bundle (air-gap export)
versions.rollbackBodySuffix: + knowledge files are reset to the snapshot state. Plugin pins stay unchanged.
versions.rollbackTypeHint: Type the first 12 characters of the bundle hash to confirm.

install.publishLeadPrefix: This surface will be written to the plugin store. After “Publish”,
install.publishLeadSuffix: is available to every agent in the tenant.
install.noSkillPromptSlot: No dedicated skill-prompt slot — the agent runs with the boilerplate default instruction.
install.sectionSetupFieldsSubtitle: to be filled in by the operator on activation
install.hintConflict: Delete the existing plugin in the store or change `id` / `version` in the spec.
install.hintBuildFailed: Go back to the slot editor and fix the spots the compiler flags.
install.hintCodegenFailed: The spec has missing required fields or unresolved placeholders — see the issue list.
install.hintSpecInvalid: The spec has a schema problem. Check required fields in the spec editor.
install.hintTooLarge: Reduce slot content or asset sizes — the server limit has been reached.
install.bumpInvalidSemver: Version '{version}' is not semver — adjust it manually in the spec editor.
install.typescriptErrors: {count, plural, one {# TypeScript error} other {# TypeScript errors}}
install.codegenIssues: {count, plural, one {# codegen issue} other {# codegen issues}}
install.successBody: is visible in the store. Redirecting …

common.missingRequiredFields: {count, plural, one {# required field missing} other {# required fields missing}}

list.headline: Build new agents.
list.lede: Create drafts, iterate on several agents in parallel and install them into the platform with one click. Every draft lives in your personal workspace — nobody sees your drafts but you.

draft.headline: No drafts yet.
draft.hint: Click “New agent” in the top right to create your first draft.

published.headline: Nothing published yet.
published.hint: Published agents appear here once you have pushed a draft into the platform via the workspace.

deleted.headline: Trash is empty.
deleted.hint: Deleted drafts show up here and can be restored until you delete them permanently.

create.label: New agent
create.quotaReachedTooltip: Draft quota reached — delete an existing one before creating a new draft.

modal.title: Import bundle
modal.description: Drop a profile bundle ZIP (export from the Versions tab via ?format=bundle) here — we automatically create a new draft.
modal.dropzone: Drag a ZIP here or click
modal.overwriteLabel: Overwrite existing live content (only meaningful for profile targets — builder drafts are always fresh).

errors.conflict: Profile already has diverging live content ({assets}). Enable “Overwrite” to replace it.
